Home CKAD exam 1
CKAD exam 1

CKAD exam 1

CKAD Exam Instructions

Before starting the exam, you must have a working MiniKube cluster. You must be able to add MiniKube nodes.

Read all questions carefully.

You can navigate through questions using the Next/Previous buttons.

The bar at the top of the screen will show you the rough amount of time remaining.

Create 3 Pods with names nginx1 📋 Copied! , nginx2 📋 Copied! , and nginx3 📋 Copied! in the namespace 104ab4 📋 Copied! running nginx:latest 📋 Copied! .

  • Label nginx1 with app=nginx 📋 Copied! .
  • Annotate nginx2 with ingress=enabled 📋 Copied! .
  • Completely unlabel the nginx3 Pod.

You are a member of team-blue and must prepare a compute node designed only for team-blue deployments.

  • Create a new minikube Node named blue 📋 Copied! , with the command minikube node add blue 📋 Copied! .
  • Add a taint to the blue 📋 Copied! node that avoids scheduling any Pods on that node.
  • Create a Deployment named blue-dedicated-node 📋 Copied! in the namespace team-blue 📋 Copied! that tolerates the taint on the blue 📋 Copied! node and is scheduled on that node.

Team Cr4zy has requested their image to be deployed on the team-cr4zy 📋 Copied! namespace. They require their image webapp-color:v1 📋 Copied! to be deployed as a container in a Deployment named cr4zy-app 📋 Copied! . The deployment should update its Pods using the Recreate 📋 Copied! strategy. As the load on the application can vary drastically during the day, you suggest using horizontal autoscaling.

Configure the cr4zy-app 📋 Copied! Deployment with horizontal autoscaling. Configure the autoscaler to deploy between 2-5 Pods, with a target CPU utilization of 75%.
